patterns.name: Hashing
patterns.do: One pass, store counts / indices in a dict or set.
patterns.gotcha: Trading space for speed — confirm O(n) memory is OK.

patterns.name: Two pointers
patterns.do: Converge from both ends, or run two same-direction pointers.
patterns.gotcha: Skip equal neighbors to avoid duplicate triplets (3Sum).

patterns.name: Sliding window
patterns.do: Grow right; shrink left while the window breaks the constraint.
patterns.gotcha: Decide fixed vs variable size, and the exact shrink condition.

patterns.name: Stack
patterns.do: Push unresolved items; pop on match. Monotonic stack for next-greater.
patterns.gotcha: For next-greater, store indices, not values.

patterns.name: Binary search
patterns.do: Halve the space; or binary-search the answer when feasibility is monotonic.
patterns.gotcha: Pick one convention (lo≤hi vs lo<hi) and keep mid moves consistent.

patterns.name: Intervals
patterns.do: Sort by start (sometimes end), then sweep comparing to the previous.
patterns.gotcha: Overlap test is prev.end > curr.start (mind ≥ vs >).

patterns.name: Linked list
patterns.do: Use dummy head, fast/slow pointers, or in-place reversal.
patterns.use: Dummy node, Floyd's cycle detection, prev/curr pointers
patterns.gotcha: Save next before reversing; handle odd-length lists for middle.

patterns.name: DFS
patterns.do: Recurse / explicit stack. Decide what to return up vs pass down.
patterns.gotcha: Graphs need a visited set; trees usually don't.

patterns.name: BFS
patterns.do: Queue, process layer by layer; first time you reach a node is shortest.
patterns.gotcha: Mark visited when enqueuing, not when dequeuing.

patterns.name: Topological sort
patterns.do: Kahn's: repeatedly remove zero-indegree nodes; or DFS post-order.
patterns.gotcha: If not all nodes get ordered, there's a cycle → impossible.

patterns.name: Union-Find
patterns.do: parent[] with union-by-rank + path compression.
patterns.gotcha: Cycle found when union of two already-joined nodes fails.

patterns.name: Weighted shortest path
patterns.do: Dijkstra (non-negative). Bellman-Ford if negative edges / K-stops limit.
patterns.gotcha: Dijkstra breaks with negative weights — switch algorithm.

patterns.name: Trie
patterns.do: Character tree, end-of-word flag on terminal nodes.
patterns.gotcha: Board search (Word Search II) = Trie + DFS together.

patterns.name: Backtracking
patterns.do: Choose → recurse → undo. Prune branches that can't succeed.
patterns.gotcha: Append a copy of the path to results, not the live list.

patterns.name: Heap / priority queue
patterns.do: Keep a size-K heap; two heaps (max+min) for a running median.
patterns.gotcha: For K largest, keep a min-heap of size K.

patterns.name: Greedy
patterns.do: Sort, then commit to the locally best choice each step.
patterns.gotcha: If you can't argue the local choice is safe, it's probably DP.

patterns.name: 1-D DP
patterns.do: Define dp[i] from earlier states; memoize, then go bottom-up.
patterns.gotcha: Get the state definition + base case right before the recurrence.

patterns.name: 2-D DP
patterns.do: Fill a dp[i][j] table from its neighbors (often i-1, j-1).
patterns.gotcha: Seed the base row/column; watch off-by-one on string lengths.

patterns.name: Math & geometry
patterns.do: Use math identities, layer-by-layer traversal, or coordinate hashing.
patterns.use: Matrix indices, fast exponentiation, hash map of points
patterns.gotcha: Watch overflow on pow/reverse integer; in-place matrix needs cycle tracking.

patterns.name: Bit manipulation
patterns.do: XOR, AND/OR masks, shifts.
patterns.gotcha: XOR cancels pairs; n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit.

triage.title: Name the input.
triage.detail: Array/string? Tree? Graph/grid? Intervals? A number? Sorted? Duplicates?

triage.title: Name the verb.
triage.detail: Find / count ways / optimize (min·max) / generate all / order / check feasible — the verb names the family.

triage.title: Read the constraints.
triage.detail: n tells you the target Big-O (table), which rules patterns in or out.

triage.title: State brute force out loud,
triage.detail: then cut repeated work: a hash map, a sort, two pointers, or caching (DP).

triage.title: Confirm before coding.
triage.detail: Restate approach + complexity to the interviewer, then write.

rescue: Would a hash map kill the nested loop?
What does the constraint size hint at?
